words.def: the process of discovering or noticing something, especially something that is not easy to see, hear, etc.; the fact of being discovered or noticed
words.example: crime prevention and detection

words.def: the state of being kept in a place, especially a prison, and prevented from leaving
words.example: They were sentenced to 12 months' detention in a young offender institution.

words.def: to become worse
words.example: Her health deteriorated rapidly, and she died shortly afterwards.

words.def: to completely destroy a place or an area
words.example: The bomb devastated much of the old part of the city.

words.def: the most powerful evil being
words.example: belief in the Devil

words.def: to invent something new or a new way of doing something
words.example: A new system has been devised to control traffic in the city.

words.def: to say exactly what an illness or the cause of a problem is
words.example: diagnose something, The test is used to diagnose a variety of diseases.

words.def: the act of discovering or identifying the exact cause of an illness or a problem
words.example: a diagnosis of lung cancer

words.def: to tell somebody what to do, especially in an annoying way
words.example: dictate something (to somebody), They are in no position to dictate terms (= tell other people what to do).

words.def: a political leader who has complete power over a country, especially one who has gained it using military force
words.example: The country suffered at the hands of a series of military dictators.

words.def: to recognize or show that two things are not the same
words.example: differentiate (between) A and B, It's difficult to differentiate between the two varieties.

words.def: a calm and serious manner that deserves respect
words.example: She accepted the criticism with quiet dignity.

words.def: a situation that makes problems, often one in which you have to make a very difficult choice between things of equal importance
words.example: I could see no way of resolving this moral dilemma.

words.def: a measurement in space, for example how high, wide or long something is
words.example: We measured the dimensions of the kitchen.

words.def: to become smaller, weaker, etc.; to make something become smaller, weaker, etc.
words.example: The world's resources are rapidly diminishing.

words.def: to put something quickly into a liquid and take it out again
words.example: dip something (into something), He dipped the brush into the paint.

words.def: a person whose job is to represent his or her country in a foreign country, for example, in an embassy
words.example: Washington's top diplomat in Havana

words.def: connected with managing relations between countries (= diplomacy)
words.example: a diplomatic crisis

words.def: a book or electronic resource containing lists of information, usually in alphabetical order, for example people’s phone numbers or the names and addresses of businesses in a particular area
words.example: a telephone/trade directory

words.def: very bad, harmful or unsuccessful
words.example: a disastrous harvest/fire/result

words.def: to get rid of something that you no longer want or need
words.example: discard somebody/something, The room was littered with discarded newspapers.

words.def: to give somebody official permission to leave the police or the armed forces; to make somebody leave the police or the armed forces
words.example: be discharged from something, He was discharged from the army following his injury.

words.def: to give somebody information about something, especially something that was previously secret
words.example: disclose something (to somebody), The spokesman refused to disclose details of the takeover to the press.

words.def: the act of making something known or public that was previously secret or private
words.example: the newspaper’s disclosure of defence secrets

words.def: a long and serious treatment or discussion of a subject in speech or writing
words.example: discourse on something, a discourse on issues of gender and sexuality

words.def: the freedom or power to decide what should be done in a particular situation
words.example: I'll leave it up to you to use your discretion.

words.def: the practice of treating somebody or a particular group in society less fairly than others
words.example: age/racial/gender/sex discrimination (= because of somebody’s age, race or sex)

words.def: the act of dismissing somebody from their job; an example of this
words.example: He still hopes to win his claim against unfair dismissal.

words.def: to take the place of somebody/something
words.example: be displaced (by somebody/something), Gradually factory workers have been displaced by machines.

words.def: the act of getting rid of something
words.example: The council is responsible for waste disposal and street cleaning.

words.def: to arrange things or people in a particular way or position
words.example: The visitors disposed themselves in a circle round the statue.

words.def: an argument between two people, groups or countries; discussion about a subject on which people disagree
words.example: industrial/pay disputes

words.def: to make it difficult for something to continue in the normal way
words.example: Demonstrators succeeded in disrupting the meeting.

words.def: a situation in which it is difficult for something to continue in the normal way; the act of stopping something from continuing in the normal way
words.example: We aim to help you move house with minimum disruption to yourself.

words.def: to mix with a liquid and become part of it
words.example: Salt dissolves in water.

words.def: a clear difference or contrast especially between people or things that are similar or related
words.example: distinctions between traditional and modern societies

words.def: having a quality or characteristic that makes something different and easily noticed
words.example: clothes with a distinctive style

words.def: to change the shape, appearance or sound of something so that it is strange or not clear
words.example: a fairground mirror that distorts your shape

words.def: a feeling of great worry or unhappiness; great mental pain
words.example: The newspaper article caused the actor considerable distress.

words.def: making you feel anxious and upset or shocked
words.example: a disturbing piece of news

words.def: to make somebody/something change direction
words.example: be diverted, The course of the stream has now been diverted.

words.def: coming from or connected with God or a god
words.example: divine law/love/will

words.def: a belief or set of beliefs held and taught by a Church, a political party, etc.
words.example: the doctrine of parliamentary sovereignty

words.def: the documents that are required for something, or that give evidence or proof of something
words.example: I couldn't enter the country because I didn't have all the necessary documentation.

words.def: an area of knowledge or activity; especially one that somebody is responsible for
words.example: Financial matters are her domain.

words.def: the fact of being more important, powerful or easy to notice than somebody/something else
words.example: political/economic dominance

words.def: a person or an organization that makes a gift of money, clothes, food, etc. to a charity, etc.
words.example: international aid donors (= countries that give money, etc. to help other countries)

words.def: an amount of a medicine or a drug that is taken once, or regularly over a period of time
words.example: a high/low/lethal dose

words.def: to make something empty or dry by removing the liquid from it; to become empty or dry in this way
words.example: Drain and rinse the pasta.

words.def: to move along smoothly and slowly in water or air
words.example: Clouds drifted across the sky.

words.def: strong and powerful; having a strong influence in making something happen
words.example: Who was the driving force (= the person with the strongest influence) in the band?

words.def: to die because you have been underwater too long and you cannot breathe; to kill somebody by holding them underwater
words.example: Two children drowned after falling into the river.

words.def: having two parts or aspects
words.example: his dual role as composer and conductor

words.def: to give somebody/something a particular name, often in a humorous or critical way
words.example: The media dubbed anorexia ‘the slimming disease’.

words.def: stupid
words.example: That was a pretty dumb thing to do.

words.def: two people who perform together or are often seen or thought of together
words.example: the comedy duo Laurel and Hardy

words.def: having a lot of energy and a strong personality
words.example: a dynamic leader

words.def: very interested and excited by something that is going to happen or about something that you want to do; showing this
words.example: eager crowds outside the stadium

words.def: the money that you earn for the work that you do
words.example: a rise in average earnings

words.def: lack of difficulty
words.example: with ease, He passed the exam with ease.

words.def: the reflecting of sound off a wall or inside a particular space so that a noise appears to be repeated; a sound that is reflected back in this way
words.example: There was an echo on the phone and I couldn't hear clearly.

words.def: connected with the relation of plants and living creatures to each other and to their environment
words.example: We risk upsetting the ecological balance of the area.

words.def: a person whose job is to teach or educate people
words.example: adult educators (= who teach adults)

words.def: the fact of producing the result that is wanted or intended; the fact of producing a successful result
words.example: to check the effectiveness of the security system

words.def: the quality of doing something well with no waste of time or money
words.example: improvements in efficiency at the factory

words.def: your sense of your own value and importance
words.example: He has the biggest ego of anyone I've ever met.

words.def: very complicated and detailed; carefully prepared and organized
words.example: elaborate designs

words.def: connected with elections
words.example: electoral systems/reforms

words.def: to give somebody/something a higher position or rank, often more important than they deserve
words.example: elevate somebody/something (to something), He elevated many of his friends to powerful positions within the government.

words.def: a person who is eligible for something or to do something, is able to have or do it because they have the right qualifications, are the right age, etc.
words.example: eligible (for something), Only those over 70 are eligible for the special payment.

words.def: a small group of people in a society, etc. who are powerful and have a lot of influence, because they are rich, intelligent, etc.
words.example: a member of the ruling/intellectual elite

words.def: to get onto a ship or plane; to put somebody/something onto a ship or plane
words.example: We stood on the pier and watched as they embarked.

words.def: shy, uncomfortable or guilty feelings; a feeling of being embarrassed
words.example: I nearly died of embarrassment when he said that.

words.def: a group of officials led by an ambassador who represent their government in a foreign country
words.example: embassy officials

words.def: to fix something in a substance or solid object
words.example: be embedded in something, an operation to remove glass that was embedded in his leg

words.def: to express or represent an idea or a quality
words.example: embody something, a politician who embodied the hopes of black youth

words.def: the fact of somebody/something moving out of or away from something and becoming possible to see
words.example: the island’s emergence from the sea 3 000 years ago

words.def: based on experiments or experience rather than ideas or theories
words.example: empirical evidence/knowledge/research

words.def: to give somebody the power or authority to do something
words.example: be empowered (to do something), The courts were empowered to impose the death sentence for certain crimes.

words.def: to pass a law
words.example: (be) enacted (by somebody/something), legislation enacted by parliament

words.def: to include a large number or range of things
words.example: The job encompasses a wide range of responsibilities.

words.def: the act of encouraging somebody to do something; something that encourages somebody
words.example: a few words of encouragement

words.def: that gives somebody support, courage or hope
words.example: This month's unemployment figures are not very encouraging.

words.def: an attempt to do something, especially something new or difficult
words.example: There have been great advances in the field of scientific endeavour.

words.def: very large in size or amount and seeming to have no end
words.example: endless patience

words.def: to say publicly that you support a person, statement or course of action
words.example: I wholeheartedly endorse his remarks.

words.def: a public statement or action showing that you support somebody/something
words.example: The election victory is a clear endorsement of their policies.

words.def: to experience and deal with something that is painful or unpleasant without giving up
words.example: endure something, They had to endure a long wait before the case came to trial.

words.def: to make sure that people obey a particular law or rule
words.example: enforce something, It's the job of the police to enforce the law.

words.def: the act of making people obey a particular law or rule
words.example: strict enforcement of regulations

words.def: an agreement to marry somebody; the period during which two people are engaged
words.example: Their engagement was announced in the local paper.

words.def: interesting or pleasant in a way that attracts your attention
words.example: an engaging smile

words.def: to ask somebody for some information
words.example: enquire about somebody/something, I called the station to enquire about train times.

words.def: to improve the quality of something, often by adding something to it
words.example: The study of science has enriched all our lives.

words.def: to arrange for yourself or for somebody else to officially join a course, school, etc.
words.example: You need to enrol before the end of August.

words.def: to happen after or as a result of another event
words.example: An argument ensued.

words.def: a company or business
words.example: He is in charge of an enterprise with a turnover of $26 billion.

words.def: a person who is very interested in something and spends a lot of time doing it
words.example: a football enthusiast

words.def: to give somebody the right to have or to do something
words.example: be entitled to something, You will be entitled to your pension when you reach 65.

words.def: something that exists separately from other things and has its own identity
words.example: The unit has become part of a larger department and no longer exists as a separate entity.

words.def: a large number of cases of a particular disease or medical condition happening at the same time in a particular community
words.example: the outbreak of a flu epidemic

words.def: the fact of being equal in rights, status, advantages, etc.
words.example: racial/social/gender equality

words.def: a statement showing that two amounts or values are equal, for example 2x + y = 54
words.example: the numbers on the right-hand side of the equation

words.def: to build something
words.example: The church was erected in 1582.

words.def: to become greater, worse, more serious, etc.; to make something greater, worse, more serious, etc.
words.example: the escalating costs of healthcare

words.def: the most important quality or feature of something, that makes it what it is
words.example: His paintings capture the essence of France.

words.def: an organization, a large institution or a hotel
words.example: The visa is for foreign nationals wishing to study at a university, college or similar educational establishment.

words.def: without an end; existing or continuing forever
words.example: the promise of eternal life in heaven

words.def: to move people from a place of danger to a safer place
words.example: evacuate something, Police evacuated nearby buildings.

words.def: to bring a feeling, a memory or an image into your mind
words.example: The music evoked memories of her youth.

words.def: connected with evolution; connected with slow steady development and change
words.example: evolutionary theory

words.def: to make something seem larger, better, worse or more important than it really is
words.example: The hotel was really filthy and I'm not exaggerating.

words.def: the quality of being extremely good
words.example: a reputation for academic excellence

words.def: unusually good
words.example: At the age of five he showed exceptional talent as a musician.

words.def: in addition to an amount that is necessary, usual or legal
words.example: Excess food is stored as fat.

words.def: the act of preventing somebody/something from entering a place or taking part in something
words.example: exclusion (of somebody/something) (from something), He was disappointed with his exclusion from the England squad.

words.def: only to be used by one particular person or group; only given to one particular person or group
words.example: The hotel has exclusive access to the beach.

words.def: for only one particular person, group or use
words.example: The resort caters almost exclusively for a high-society public.

words.def: to kill somebody, especially as a legal punishment
words.example: be executed (for something), He was executed for treason.

words.def: the act of killing somebody, especially as a legal punishment
words.example: He faced execution by hanging for murder.
